title: xlsread
summary: Read numeric, text, and raw data from spreadsheet files.

gpu_support.notes: Spreadsheet files are read on the host. Results are CPU-resident values.

description: `xlsread` imports spreadsheet data using MATLAB's legacy call forms. The first output is a dense double matrix; optional second and third outputs return text cells and raw cells.
behaviors: `xlsread(filename)` reads the first worksheet. If the filename has no extension, `.xlsx` is appended.
`xlsread(filename, range)` reads an Excel A1 range such as `"C4:C19"` from the first worksheet.
`xlsread(filename, sheet)` selects a worksheet by name or one-based numeric index.
`xlsread(filename, sheet, range)` combines worksheet and range selection.
The legacy `'-basic'` mode flag is accepted as a no-op because RunMat always uses its host spreadsheet parser rather than Excel COM automation.
The numeric output trims leading and trailing rows/columns that contain no numeric cells, then fills nonnumeric cells inside the numeric rectangle with `NaN`.
`[num, txt, raw] = xlsread(...)` returns text cells in `txt` and mixed number/string/logical cells in `raw`.
Excel callback/process-function modes are not supported and raise a descriptive argument error.

examples.description: Read a named worksheet and range
Capture numeric, text, and raw outputs

faqs.question: Which spreadsheet formats are supported?
faqs.answer: RunMat uses the same spreadsheet reader as `readtable`, covering common Excel/OpenDocument formats handled by the runtime parser, including `.xls`, `.xlsx`, `.xlsm`, `.xlsb`, and `.ods`.

faqs.question: Does `xlsread` use Microsoft Excel?
faqs.answer: No. RunMat reads spreadsheets directly on the host. The `'-basic'` compatibility flag is accepted, but COM automation and Excel callback processing are not part of RunMat's runtime model.

faqs.question: What should I use for new code?
faqs.answer: `xlsread` exists for legacy MATLAB compatibility. For new mixed-type imports, prefer `readtable`; for numeric matrices, `readmatrix` is the modern MATLAB API.

gpu_residency: `xlsread` always creates host values. Move the numeric output to the GPU explicitly with `gpuArray` if later computation should run on the active acceleration provider.
gpu_behavior: `xlsread` performs file I/O and spreadsheet parsing on the CPU. It is not fusible and does not call acceleration provider hooks.
